description: Advanced physics problems with comprehensive step-by-step solutions covering all Grade 11 topics. Master mechanics, thermodynamics, and electromagnetism through rigorous practice.

topics.name: Unit 1: Foundations & Measurement
topics.description: SI units, significant figures, dimensional analysis, error analysis, scientific notation, and mathematical foundations for physics

topics.name: Unit 2: 1D Kinematics
topics.description: Position, velocity, acceleration, motion graphs, kinematic equations, free fall, and analysis of motion in one dimension

topics.name: Unit 3: 2D Kinematics
topics.description: Vector analysis, projectile motion, relative motion, and motion in two dimensions with advanced problem-solving

topics.name: Unit 4: Newton's Laws
topics.description: Force analysis, free-body diagrams, tension, pulleys, friction, and multi-body systems applying Newton's laws of motion

topics.name: Unit 5: Circular Motion & Gravity
topics.description: Centripetal force, angular motion, universal gravitation, orbital mechanics, and Kepler's laws

topics.name: Unit 6: Work, Energy & Power
topics.description: Work-energy theorem, kinetic and potential energy, conservation of mechanical energy, power, and efficiency calculations

topics.name: Unit 7: Momentum & Impulse
topics.description: Linear momentum, impulse-momentum theorem, conservation of momentum, elastic and inelastic collisions in 1D and 2D

topics.name: Unit 8: Waves & Sound
topics.description: Wave properties, superposition, interference, standing waves, Doppler effect, resonance, and sound intensity

topics.name: Unit 9: Electricity & Circuits
topics.description: Ohm's law, series and parallel circuits, Kirchhoff's rules, circuit analysis, resistivity, and electrical power

topics.name: Unit 10: Modern Physics
topics.description: Quantum theory, photoelectric effect, atomic models, nuclear decay, mass-energy equivalence, and introduction to relativity
